front: Who was Gwilym M. Jenkins and what is he best known for in the field of statistics?
back: Gwilym M. Jenkins was a British statistician best known as the co-author of the seminal 1970 book 'Time Series Analysis: Forecasting and Control,' which introduced the widely-used Box-Jenkins methodology for ARIMA models.

front: What is the name of the influential book on time series co-authored by Jenkins and what did it introduce?
back: The book is 'Time Series Analysis: Forecasting and Control' (1970). It introduced the Box-Jenkins methodology, a comprehensive framework for identifying, fitting, and checking autoregressive integrated moving average (ARIMA) models.

front: What are the three main stages of the Box-Jenkins methodology for building ARIMA models?
back: 1. Identification: Using tools like ACF and PACF plots to tentatively identify the model order (p, d, q). 2. Estimation: Fitting the model parameters to the data. 3. Diagnostic Checking: Analyzing the residuals to verify the model is adequate (e.g., ensuring residuals are white noise).

front: With which other famous statistician did Jenkins collaborate to create his most famous work?
back: Gwilym Jenkins collaborated with George E. P. Box. Their partnership is immortalized in the name of the Box-Jenkins methodology and the general class of models known as ARIMA (AutoRegressive Integrated Moving Average).

front: Beyond the Box-Jenkins methodology, what significant contribution did Jenkins make to the field of systems engineering?
back: Jenkins was a pioneer in applying statistical time series methods to problems in systems engineering. He founded the Department of Systems Engineering at Lancaster University, focusing on the interdisciplinary application of these techniques to real-world systems.
